title: sorbus scandica subsp. rupicola
intro: Sorbus scandica subsp. rupicola, also known as the Rock Whitebeam, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Mountain Ash tree, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits associated with Sorbus scandica subsp. rupicola include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or infused in hot water to make a medicinal infusion, often combined with other herbs for enhanced therapeutic effects.
